task_completion.description: Navigate to the target project directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal prompt or file manager showing the current path as '/home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor/'.

task_completion.description: Open the Docker configuration files for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) open, displaying the contents of 'docker-compose.yml' or 'Dockerfile'.

task_completion.description: Modify the configuration to ensure Python logs stream in real-time without buffering.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the addition of an environment variable like 'PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1' (or 'PYTHONUNBUFFERED: 1' in YAML) or modifying the command to use 'python -u'.

task_completion.description: Modify the configuration to include an init process to handle system signals for graceful shutdown.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the addition of 'init: true' in docker-compose.yml, or the installation/usage of an init system like 'tini' or 'dumb-init' in the Dockerfile.

task_completion.description: Rebuild or recreate the Docker container to apply the new infrastructure configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of commands like 'docker compose up -d', 'docker compose up --force-recreate', or 'docker compose build' completing successfully.

task_completion.description: Test the container to ensure it shuts down quickly and logs stream properly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker compose down' or 'docker stop order-processor' completing in just a few seconds, and/or 'docker logs -f order-processor' showing streaming output.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not modify the legacy Python script to solve the issue.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not open or edit any .py files (e.g., app.py, main.py) to add signal handlers or sys.stdout.flush(). All changes must be in Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually applied the changes using Docker CLI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual execution of 'docker' or 'docker compose' commands in the terminal, rather than just editing files without applying them.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent modified the files in the correct project directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths in the editor or terminal commands must point to '/home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory is '/home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor'. Container name is 'order-processor'. The agent must configure an init process (e.g., 'init: true' in docker-compose.yml) for graceful shutdown and unbuffered Python execution (e.g., 'PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1' or 'python -u') for real-time logs. A successful shutdown takes under 4.0 seconds; taking 9.0 seconds or more is considered a timeout.
